title: Implementing a Database-Driven Resource Server with Rate Limiting
statement: Create an Express.js server that connects to an in-memory SQLite database and handles CRUD operations for users. Implement rate limiting to prevent excessive requests. Ensure API endpoints are properly secured via authentication (using basic auth) and handle edge cases. Expose endpoints for user creation, retrieval, and deletion. Use the provided starter code as a foundation.
constraints: Must use an in-memory SQLite database provided by the `sqlite3` package
Must implement rate limiting using the `express-rate-limit` package

endpoints.description: Retrieve a list of users in the database. Supports pagination (page and limit query parameters). Returns a JSON array of user objects.
Retrieve a user by their ID. Returns a single user object or 404 if not found.
Create a new user. Requires a JSON body with `username` and `password` properties. Returns the created user ID on success.
Delete a user by their ID. Returns a success message on success or 404 if not found.
